original_hypothesis.title: Blacklisted ADMIN Bypasses Sender Blacklist on Standard Transfers
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: adminSanity() exempts ADMIN callers from both pause and sender blacklist checks. If an ADMIN address is blacklisted (e.g., after key compromise), the blacklisted ADMIN can still call transfer() and transferFrom() to move their tokens out, completely bypassing the blacklist mechanism designed to freeze their funds.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The blacklist is intended as an absolute freeze on an address. However, ADMIN callers skip the sender blacklist check, meaning a compromised ADMIN can exfiltrate funds even after being blacklisted. This creates a window where the OWNER must both blacklist AND revoke ADMIN role before the compromised admin can move tokens.

proof_of_signal.pattern: adminSanity: if (!hasRole(ADMIN, _msgSender())) { if (isBlacklisted(from)) revert; } — ADMIN callers skip sender blacklist check entirely

preconditions: OWNER or another ADMIN blacklists address A
A still holds ADMIN role (not yet revoked)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: isBlacklisted(A) == true, hasRole(ADMIN, A) == true → adminSanity skips blacklist → transfer succeeds
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Compromised ADMIN moves all tokens out before OWNER can revoke their role
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: ADMIN extracts full balance despite being blacklisted

economic_questions: Can OWNER atomically blacklist + revoke ADMIN in one transaction?
What is the ADMIN's max balance at risk?
Does this affect forceTransfer as well?
validation_requirements: Test: blacklist(adminAddr) then admin calls transfer() - confirm success
Test: admin can also call transferFrom while blacklisted
Verify no other check prevents blacklisted admin from transferring

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: None for transfer; DEX liquidity to sell
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after blacklisting, before role revocation

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm ADMIN bypass of sender blacklist

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
